task_completion.description: Access the cluster and locate the sales-prod namespace.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Rancher UI showing the 'sales-prod' namespace or terminal showing a kubectl command targeting '-n sales-prod'.

task_completion.description: Deploy an ephemeral container to the order-processor pod.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a command like 'kubectl debug -it order-processor' or Rancher UI showing an ephemeral container being added to the pod.

task_completion.description: Read the trace.json file from the main-app container.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing the contents of '/app/diagnostics/trace.json' (often accessed via /proc/<pid>/root/app/... from the debug container) and revealing the 'fault_signature' value.

task_completion.description: Create a Kubernetes Secret named fault-signature-hotfix.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Rancher UI Secret creation page or terminal showing 'kubectl create secret' with the name 'fault-signature-hotfix' in the 'sales-prod' namespace.

task_completion.description: Add the 'signature' key with the extracted fault signature to the Secret.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Rancher UI showing the Secret data with key 'signature' and the corresponding extracted string value, or terminal showing the exact key-value pair being applied to the secret.

integrity.description: The agent must not delete, recreate, or copy the order-processor pod.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal commands showing 'kubectl delete pod order-processor' or 'kubectl replace'. The pod UID and age should remain consistent if visible.

integrity.description: The agent must extract the data using an ephemeral container, not by guessing or using standard exec/cp.
integrity.visual_evidence: Evidence of 'kubectl debug' or ephemeral container usage. Standard 'kubectl exec' or 'kubectl cp' commands should either not be present or show as failed.

integrity.description: The secret must be created in the actual Kubernetes environment.
integrity.visual_evidence: The secret creation must happen via Rancher UI or a valid kubectl command in the terminal, not just written in a local text file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target namespace: 'sales-prod'. Target pod: 'order-processor'. Target secret name: 'fault-signature-hotfix'. The agent must extract the 'fault_signature' from '/app/diagnostics/trace.json' using an ephemeral container (e.g., kubectl debug) without recreating the pod, and store it in the secret under the key 'signature'.
